verdict_reason: The candidate possesses strong general software engineering and leadership skills but completely lacks the mandatory and highly specialized automotive embedded software experience, including Embedded C/C++, AUTOSAR, and functional safety standards. The core technical requirements for this role are entirely unmet, necessitating a review to confirm if any transferable skills could be considered, though it's highly unlikely for such a specialized role.

cleaned_narrative:
This candidate presents as a highly experienced Lead Software Engineer with approximately 9 years of professional experience, complemented by an internship. Their expertise is primarily centered around Python and Go programming, cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, Open Stack), DevOps tools (Docker, Kubernetes, Jenkins, Ansible), API development, and machine learning. They have a strong track record in leading teams, managing projects, and collaborating with senior stakeholders to enhance products, demonstrating solid leadership and communication skills.

However, the candidate's profile exhibits a significant mismatch with the core requirements of the Senior Automotive Embedded Software Engineer role. The job description explicitly demands deep expertise in automotive embedded software, Embedded C/C++, AUTOSAR, specific automotive microcontrollers like Infineon Aurix, and adherence to automotive industry standards such as ISO 26262 and Automotive SPICE. The candidate's background is almost entirely in enterprise software, cloud infrastructure, and general software development, with no mention of any automotive, embedded, or C/C++ experience.

Given the fundamental divergence in technical domain and required programming languages, the candidate is not a suitable fit for this highly specialized automotive embedded role. The critical mandatory skills are entirely absent from their profile.
key_strengths: Extensive Software Engineering Experience**: Over 9 years of professional experience in software development, demonstrating a long-term commitment to the field.

Strong Leadership and Team Management**: Proven ability to lead teams, manage staff, and collaborate with senior management, as evidenced by roles like "Senior Consultant & Team Lead" and responsibilities like "Leading the team to meet the deadlines."

Proficiency in Cloud & DevOps Technologies**: Deep expertise in modern cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, Open Stack) and DevOps tools (Docker, Kubernetes, Jenkins, Ansible), indicating strong skills in scalable system development and deployment.
Diverse Programming Skillset**: Proficient in Python and Go, with experience in developing APIs and utilizing machine learning algorithms.
potential_concerns: Lack of Embedded C/C++ Expertise**: The most critical gap is the complete absence of Embedded C/C++ experience, which is a mandatory and foundational skill for this role. This makes the candidate unsuitable for the position.
No Automotive Domain Experience**: The candidate lacks any mentioned experience in the automotive industry or embedded systems, which is a mandatory requirement for the role's specific context.

Missing AUTOSAR and Microcontroller Experience**: There is no evidence of experience with AUTOSAR architecture, integration, or specific automotive microcontrollers like Infineon Aurix, which are mandatory for the role.

Absence of Functional Safety & Quality Standards Knowledge**: The CV does not mention ISO 26262, Automotive SPICE, or other automotive safety/quality standards, all of which are mandatory for developing safety-critical automotive systems.

detailed_analysis:
SECTION 1 – Overall Assessment:
This candidate presents as a highly experienced Lead Software Engineer with approximately 9 years of professional experience, complemented by an internship. Their expertise is primarily centered around Python and Go programming, cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, Open Stack), DevOps tools (Docker, Kubernetes, Jenkins, Ansible), API development, and machine learning. They have a strong track record in leading teams, managing projects, and collaborating with senior stakeholders to enhance products, demonstrating solid leadership and communication skills.

However, the candidate's profile exhibits a significant mismatch with the core requirements of the Senior Automotive Embedded Software Engineer role. The job description explicitly demands deep expertise in automotive embedded software, Embedded C/C++, AUTOSAR, specific automotive microcontrollers like Infineon Aurix, and adherence to automotive industry standards such as ISO 26262 and Automotive SPICE. The candidate's background is almost entirely in enterprise software, cloud infrastructure, and general software development, with no mention of any automotive, embedded, or C/C++ experience.

Given the fundamental divergence in technical domain and required programming languages, the candidate is not a suitable fit for this highly specialized automotive embedded role. The critical mandatory skills are entirely absent from their profile.

SECTION 2 – Fitment Analysis Table:
CATEGORY: Total Experience
JD_REQUIRES: 5+ years of automotive embedded software development experience
CANDIDATE_HAS: Approximately 9 years of professional software engineering experience (excluding internship). "Lead Software Engineer with 10+ years of Experience" stated on CV.
MATCH_STATUS: PARTIAL_MATCH (Meets general experience length, but not specific domain experience)

CATEGORY: Core Programming Languages
JD_REQUIRES: Expert-level proficiency in Embedded C and C++
CANDIDATE_HAS: Python, Go, Node JS. No mention of C or C++.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH

CATEGORY: Primary Domain / Industry
JD_REQUIRES: Automotive embedded software development
CANDIDATE_HAS: Experience in enterprise software, cloud platforms (Open Shift, AWS, Azure, Open Stack), APIs, and machine learning. No mention of automotive or embedded systems.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH

CATEGORY: Frameworks & Tools (Automotive Specific)
JD_REQUIRES: Strong AUTOSAR architecture and integration experience, Experience with Infineon Aurix or similar automotive microcontrollers, CAN/CAN-FD protocol expertise
CANDIDATE_HAS: Python, APIs, Flask, Django, Machine Learning, Ansible, Jenkins, Docker, Kubernetes, AWS, Azure, Open Stack, Linux, Windows, Go, MySQL, MongoDB, Cassandra, Node JS, REST APIs, Open Shift, Pandas. No mention of AUTOSAR, Infineon Aurix, CAN/CAN-FD, or similar automotive tools/frameworks.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH

CATEGORY: Architecture & Design Patterns
JD_REQUIRES: AUTOSAR architecture design and integration
CANDIDATE_HAS: "Design patterns" listed in skills. No specific mention of AUTOSAR architecture.
MATCH_STATUS: PARTIAL_MATCH (General design patterns, but not specific to AUTOSAR)

CATEGORY: Cloud / Infrastructure
JD_REQUIRES: Not explicitly required, but the role is embedded.
CANDIDATE_HAS: Strong experience with AWS, Azure, Open Stack, Docker, Kubernetes, Ansible, Linux.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH (Strong in a different domain, not relevant to embedded)

CATEGORY: Leadership & Team Management
JD_REQUIRES: Team leadership or technical architect experience (Nice to Have), lead AUTOSAR architecture design, mentor junior engineers
CANDIDATE_HAS: "Lead Software Engineer", "Senior Consultant & Team Lead", "Leading the team to meet the deadlines", "Work with Product Manager, Engineer Manager, and Directors", "Competent in staff management".
MATCH_STATUS: FULL_MATCH

CATEGORY: Functional Safety & Quality Standards
JD_REQUIRES: ISO 26262 functional safety knowledge and application, Automotive SPICE process experience
CANDIDATE_HAS: No mention of ISO 26262 or Automotive SPICE.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH

CATEGORY: Certifications (Automotive Specific)
JD_REQUIRES: ISO/SAE 21434 automotive cybersecurity certification (Nice to Have), MISRA C/C++ compliance expertise (Nice to Have)
CANDIDATE_HAS: Red Hat Certified Developer in Containerized Applications, Red Hat Certified System administrator, Crucial conversations, GO basics. No automotive-specific certifications or compliance expertise.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH

SECTION 3 – Key Strengths:
-   **Extensive Software Engineering Experience**: Over 9 years of professional experience in software development, demonstrating a long-term commitment to the field.
-   **Strong Leadership and Team Management**: Proven ability to lead teams, manage staff, and collaborate with senior management, as evidenced by roles like "Senior Consultant & Team Lead" and responsibilities like "Leading the team to meet the deadlines."
-   **Proficiency in Cloud & DevOps Technologies**: Deep expertise in modern cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, Open Stack) and DevOps tools (Docker, Kubernetes, Jenkins, Ansible), indicating strong skills in scalable system development and deployment.
-   **Diverse Programming Skillset**: Proficient in Python and Go, with experience in developing APIs and utilizing machine learning algorithms.

SECTION 4 – Potential Gaps / Areas to Verify:
-   **Lack of Embedded C/C++ Expertise**: The most critical gap is the complete absence of Embedded C/C++ experience, which is a mandatory and foundational skill for this role. This makes the candidate unsuitable for the position.
-   **No Automotive Domain Experience**: The candidate lacks any mentioned experience in the automotive industry or embedded systems, which is a mandatory requirement for the role's specific context.
-   **Missing AUTOSAR and Microcontroller Experience**: There is no evidence of experience with AUTOSAR architecture, integration, or specific automotive microcontrollers like Infineon Aurix, which are mandatory for the role.
-   **Absence of Functional Safety & Quality Standards Knowledge**: The CV does not mention ISO 26262, Automotive SPICE, or other automotive safety/quality standards, all of which are mandatory for developing safety-critical automotive systems.

SECTION 5 – Experience Breakdown:
Years of Experience: ~9 years (professional)
Seniority Level: SENIOR (closer to LEAD, but the core technical match is missing for LEAD in this specific domain)
Core Technical Skills: Python, APIs, Flask, Django, Machine Learning, Ansible, Jenkins, Docker, Kubernetes, AWS, Azure, Open Stack, Linux, Go, MySQL, MongoDB, Cassandra.
Secondary Skills: Project Management, Scrum Master, Node JS, REST APIs, Open Shift, Pandas, Windows, MAC, Critical thinking, Team-building, Staff management, Proposal development.
Primary Domain: Cloud Infrastructure, Enterprise Software, DevOps, API Development.
Leadership Indicators: "Lead Software Engineer", "Senior Consultant & Team Lead", "Leading the team to meet the deadlines", "Work with Product Manager, Engineer Manager, and Directors", "Competent in staff management".

FINAL RECOMMENDATION:
REVIEW
Confidence: 10%
Match Score: 15%

The core technical requirements for this role are entirely unmet, necessitating a review to confirm if any transferable skills could be considered, though it's highly unlikely for such a specialized role.

llm_prompt_used:
You are a senior technical recruiter performing a detailed fitment analysis. Compare the anonymized professional profile against the job description below.

IMPORTANT RULES:
- The CV is already anonymized (all PII removed). NEVER output names, emails, phone numbers, addresses, or company locations.
- If you cannot determine something, state "Not specified" — NEVER invent details.
- Be thorough and evidence-based. Cite specific technologies, years, and project details from the CV.
- Provide a DETAILED category-by-category comparison like a professional recruitment report.
- The Overall Assessment MUST be an original analytical summary you write — do NOT copy-paste text from the CV.

OUTPUT FORMAT (FOLLOW THIS STRUCTURE EXACTLY):

SECTION 1 – Overall Assessment:
[Write a 2-3 paragraph ORIGINAL executive summary in your own words covering:
- Who the candidate is (years of experience, primary role, key expertise areas)
- Why they are / are not a good fit for this specific role
- One line recommendation
IMPORTANT: This must be YOUR analytical summary, NOT copied text from the CV. Synthesize the information into a professional recruiter assessment.]

SECTION 2 – Fitment Analysis Table:
For EACH major requirement category in the JD, provide a line in this exact format:
CATEGORY: [category name]
JD_REQUIRES: [what the JD asks for]
CANDIDATE_HAS: [what the candidate actually has, with evidence]
MATCH_STATUS: [FULL_MATCH | PARTIAL_MATCH | NO_MATCH]

(Create one entry for each of these categories, adapting to the JD:
- Total Experience
- Core Programming Languages
- Primary Domain / Industry
- Frameworks & Tools
- Architecture & Design Patterns
- Cloud / Infrastructure
- Leadership & Team Management
- Education / Certifications
- Any other JD-specific categories)

SECTION 3 – Key Strengths:
- [Strength 1 with specific evidence from CV]
- [Strength 2 with specific evidence from CV]
- [Strength 3 with specific evidence from CV]
- [Strength 4 with specific evidence from CV (if applicable)]

SECTION 4 – Potential Gaps / Areas to Verify:
- [Gap 1: what is missing or unclear, and its impact]
- [Gap 2: what is missing or unclear, and its impact]
(If no gaps: "No critical gaps identified.")

SECTION 5 – Experience Breakdown:
Years of Experience: [exact number, e.g., "9 years" or "5-6 years"]
Seniority Level: [ENTRY: 0-2yrs | MID: 2-5yrs | SENIOR: 5-10yrs | LEAD: 10-15yrs | EXECUTIVE: 15+yrs]
Core Technical Skills: [List top 10 technical skills from CV]
Secondary Skills: [List additional tools, frameworks, soft skills]
Primary Domain: [Main industry/sector e.g., "Automotive Embedded", "Web Development"]
Leadership Indicators: [List concrete evidence: "Led 5-person team", "Mentored 3 juniors", or "None mentioned"]

FINAL RECOMMENDATION:
[SHORTLIST | BACKUP | REVIEW]
Confidence: [0-100]%
Match Score: [0-100]%

Reason: [2-3 sentences with specific evidence. First sentence: overall verdict with primary reason. Second: key matching evidence. Third: what tips the balance.]

===== DECISION RULES =====
- SHORTLIST: 80%+ requirements matched with strong evidence. Ready for interview.
- BACKUP: 60-79% requirements matched. Good candidate but has some gaps.
- REVIEW: <60% matched OR unclear/insufficient CV data → human must decide.
- If Confidence <70%, automatically use REVIEW regardless of match score.
- NEVER use REJECT — when in doubt, use REVIEW.

---

JOB DESCRIPTION:
Senior Automotive Embedded Software Engineer

MANDATORY REQUIREMENTS (Must Have):
• 5+ years of automotive embedded software development experience
• Expert-level proficiency in Embedded C and C++
• Strong AUTOSAR architecture and integration experience
• Experience with Infineon Aurix or similar automotive microcontrollers
• ISO 26262 functional safety knowledge and application
• Automotive SPICE process experience
• CAN/CAN-FD protocol expertise

NICE TO HAVE (Preferred):
• ISO/SAE 21434 automotive cybersecurity certification
• Electric vehicle or hybrid powertrain experience
• Battery management system (BMS) development
• Vector CANoe, CANalyzer tools
• Requirements engineering with DOORS, PTC Integrity, or CodeBeamer
• Experience with major OEMs (Mercedes, BMW, Volkswagen, etc.)
• Team leadership or technical architect experience
• MISRA C/C++ compliance expertise

ROLE DESCRIPTION:
We are seeking a senior embedded software engineer to lead AUTOSAR architecture design and integration for next-generation electric vehicle platforms. The ideal candidate will work with cross-functional teams to develop safety-critical automotive systems, conduct SPICE assessments, and mentor junior engineers.

Key responsibilities include:
- Designing and implementing AUTOSAR software components
- Conducting requirements engineering for electric powertrain systems
- Performing functional safety analysis per ISO 26262
- Integrating software on Infineon Aurix multicore platforms
- Leading technical reviews and SPICE assessments
- Collaborating with international OEM customers

This role requires strong technical expertise, excellent communication skills, and the ability to work independently in a fast-paced automotive development environment.
